<!-- Generated by the andvari-hardening-audit multi-agent workflow (run wf_14c9179d-389,
     27 agents, 20 raw findings -> 17 surviving after adversarial verification).
     Lead synthesis reviewed and filed by huginn. -->

# andvari тАФ Hardening-Gate Security Self-Audit

**Repository:** `/root/netplan/dev-ledger/andvari` (own git repo)
**Date:** 2026-07-06 ┬╖ **Milestone:** post-P4, pre-real-secrets ┬╖ **Auditor:** huginn (lead)
**Deployment under test:** CT 122 `andvari`, Tailscale-primary, break-glass public via Cloudflare Tunnel + Access; single household.

---

## 1. Scope & method

**What was audited (source, not just spec):**

- **Server** (`server/src/main/kotlin/io/silencelen/andvari/server/`) тАФ `Service.kt`, `AdminService.kt`, `AttachmentStore.kt`, `App.kt` (routing/CSP/rate limits), `Auth.kt`, `Repo.kt`, `Db.kt`, `Config.kt`, `AuditLog.kt`, plus `logback.xml` and `ops/andvari.env.example`.
- **`:core` crypto** (`core/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/silencelen/andvari/core/crypto|vault|client`) and the **web crypto** twin (`web/src/crypto`, `web/src/vault`).
- **Auth / session** тАФ prelogin, login, TOTP, refresh rotation, rate limiting, client-IP attribution.
- **Attachment surface** тАФ streamed upload, quota accounting, delete-in-batch transactionality, idempotency oracle.
- **Admin & injection surface** тАФ `/admin/*`, policy update, `/escrow/self`, sync-push authorization/audit, id validation.
- **Web client** (`web/src/ui`, `web/src/api`) тАФ token storage, WS auth, CSP.

**The invariant under test тАФ spec 02 ┬з5, "the zero-knowledge contract":** the server stores/sees *exactly* the fields in the ┬з5 table and nothing more. All vault content (item names, URIs, passwords, TOTP seeds, notes, `passwordHistory`, attachment filenames, and per-file `fileKey`s) is client-encrypted (XChaCha20-Poly1305, associated-data-bound per ┬з2) before it ever reaches CT 122. Anything vault-derived appearing server-side in plaintext is, by definition, a violation.

**Method:** each dimension was read against spec 00тАУ05 first (design intent), then against the actual code. Every candidate finding was then **adversarially verified** тАФ a second pass tried to refute it (does a rate limit, an auth gate, a guarded-CAS, an accepted-risk clause, or the concurrency model neutralize it?). Findings that were refuted or that mis-stated the code were dropped; findings whose real-world severity was overstated were downgraded to their honest level. Of 20 raw candidates, **3 were refuted** (a false break-glass Host-header claim, a login timing/DB-write oracle, and an `applyRecovery` re-enable claim) and **17 survived**. Severities below are the post-verification levels, rated for this specific single-household Tailscale/LAN deployment (with the public break-glass tunnel as the hostile-network case).

---

## 2. Verdict on the zero-knowledge claim

**The server-sees-only-ciphertext invariant HOLDS in the implementation.** This is the headline result and it is stated with evidence, not on faith:

- **Every one of the 17 surviving findings has `zkImpact: false`.** None of them causes vault plaintext тАФ names, URIs, passwords, TOTP seeds, notes, attachment filenames, or `fileKey`s тАФ to reach the server in cleartext. The findings are auth/session robustness, audit/forensic completeness, attachment integrity/availability, and web-client hygiene issues.
- **Item content is sealed client-side under VK with AD binding** (`core` vault layer + `web/src/vault/account.ts`); attachment content is `crypto_secretstream` under a per-file key that lives *inside* the item ciphertext, and `AttachmentStore` persists only `header + ciphertext + sha256(ciphertext) + size` (`AttachmentStore.kt:108`) тАФ never filename or `fileKey`. This matches the ┬з5 `attachments` row exactly.
- **Keys are never persisted in the clear on any client.** The web `Account` holds UVK/VK/identity keys in memory only (`web/src/vault/account.ts`); the *only* web persistence sink is `session.ts`, and it stores **tokens, not keys** (see LOW-9 / INFO). A leaked session yields ciphertext-sync access, never plaintext.
- **The two closest-to-the-line findings still do not breach ┬з5:** the account **email** written into audit `meta` (INFO-1) is already an explicit ┬з5 `users` field тАФ a data-minimization concern about copying it into the central Loki store, not a ZK break; and the **KDF-floor gap** (LOW-1) weakens the *resilience* of the spec 05 T8 "offline-cracking" guarantee but does not change what the server stores (still only `{kdfSalt, kdfParams, verifier, wrappedUvk}`).

Bottom line: an attacker who fully compromises CT 122 (spec 05 T1) тАФ DB, blobs, memory, logs тАФ still sees only the ┬з5 plaintext plus traffic patterns, exactly as designed. The implementation is faithful to its zero-knowledge claim.

---

## 3. Findings

Grouped by post-verification severity. **CRITICAL: none. HIGH: none. MEDIUM: none.** (Two originally-medium findings тАФ refresh-token TOCTOU and non-transactional attachment deletion тАФ were downgraded to LOW after verification; the third medium candidate, the break-glass Host-header claim, was refuted outright.) No surviving finding touches the ZK contract.

### LOW (9)

**LOW-1 тАФ No minimum floor on Argon2id KDF params; server-supplied weak params accepted verbatim**
`core/.../crypto/Keys.kt:15` (twin: `web/src/crypto/keys.ts:22`); stored verbatim at `server/.../Service.kt:82`, `Service.kt:205` (password change), `AdminService.kt:62` (admin reset).
`KdfParams.init` validates only `v==1 && alg=="argon2id13"` тАФ it never enforces a minimum `ops`/`memBytes`, despite spec 01 ┬з9's explicit hard rule *"Never go below 64 MiB memory."* All three clients take enrollment params from server-supplied `policy.kdfParams` (Android:135, Desktop:136, `web/src/ui/Welcome.tsx:200`), and the server persists whatever arrives.
*Failure scenario:* a compromised/misconfigured server serves `{ops:1, memBytes:8388608}` (8 MiB) as policy; an honest client enrolls or changes password at ~1/8 the intended cost, so the resulting `verifier` and `wrappedUvk` are weak. If the DB later leaks (spec 05 T8), the master password тЖТ wrapKey тЖТ UVK тЖТ whole vault is brute-forceable ~24├Ч cheaper than the ~64 MiB/ops=3 design assumes. Login self-protects (a verifier mismatch), but enrollment and re-key take the strength directly from server policy.
*Why it matters here:* this is the one finding that violates an explicit written hard rule and applies to the household's *imminent first real enrollment*.
*Remediation:* enforce a hard floor (`ops>=3 && memBytes>=67108864`) in `KdfParams.init` on **both** clients, and reject sub-floor params server-side in `register`/`changePassword`/admin-reset before persisting.

**LOW-2 тАФ Refresh-token rotation has a check-then-consume TOCTOU that defeats single-use and reuse-detection**
`server/.../Service.kt:232` (snapshot read at `:215`, reuse check at `:217`).
The reuse check `if (s.refreshConsumedAt != null)` runs on a snapshot read *outside* the write transaction; the consume `UPDATE sessions SET refreshConsumedAt=? WHERE sessionId=?` (line 232) is **unconditional** тАФ no `AND refreshConsumedAt IS NULL`, no affected-row check. The same file already does this correctly for TOTP at login (`Service.kt:134`: `... AND totpLastStep<?` + `if (consumed != 1)`), but the pattern was not applied here. `/auth/refresh` has no rate limiter (`App.kt:243`).
*Failure scenario:* two concurrent uses of one stolen refresh token both read `refreshConsumedAt==null`, both pass the reuse check, both mint fresh valid access+refresh chains, and **neither trips** the "reuse revokes the whole device" theft signal (spec 03 ┬з2). The audited tripwire is silently bypassed.
*Scope:* presupposes an already-stolen valid refresh token; grants no new plaintext and no new access тАФ it erodes a defense-in-depth *detection* control.
*Remediation:* one line тАФ add `AND refreshConsumedAt IS NULL` to the consume and check the affected-row count (`if (consumed != 1) тЖТ treat as reuse тЖТ revoke device`), mirroring the TOTP path.

**LOW-3 тАФ `clientIp()` collapses to loopback behind the proxies, degrading per-IP rate limiting and audit IPs**
`server/.../Auth.kt:27`.
`clientIp()` returns `request.origin.remoteHost`; no `ForwardedHeaders` plugin is installed and `CF-Connecting-IP` is never read. Both front-ends (tailscale serve, cloudflared) terminate TLS and connect over loopback, so every remote request records `127.0.0.1`. Consequences: (a) the `prelogin:127.0.0.1` / `login:127.0.0.1` rate buckets (`App.kt:223/236`) are a single global bucket on exactly the proxied paths; (b) every audit `ip` (login_fail, refresh_reuse, register, password_change) is `127.0.0.1`, degrading forensic value. Note this is *not* an XFF-spoofing bug (XFF is deliberately ignored, `App.kt:173-174`) тАФ it is the opposite: the real client IP is discarded.
*Verification note:* the flashy DoS variant was **refuted** тАФ the public hostname sits behind Cloudflare Access with a single-identity allowlist, so no anonymous attacker can reach `/auth/login` to exhaust the shared bucket. The residual is real but low: loss of per-source audit attribution and per-IP rate granularity, recoverable out-of-band via Tailscale + CF Access connection logs.
*Remediation:* on the proxied listener only, trust `CF-Connecting-IP` (public) / the tailscale-serve forwarded client (tailnet) for rate-key and audit `ip`; keep ignoring raw XFF on the LAN-fallback listener that binds `0.0.0.0`.

**LOW-4 тАФ `enumSecret` silently defaults to 32 zero bytes with no startup guard**
`server/.../Config.kt:41`; shipped empty in `ops/andvari.env.example:21`; consumed at `ServerCrypto.kt:34` / `Service.kt:53`.
`enumSecret` falls back to `ByteArray(32)` (all zeros) when `ANDVARI_ENUM_SECRET` is unset, and nothing at startup requires it тАФ the comment only says "prod MUST set one," and the example env ships it empty (which decodes to a 0-byte key without throwing). The anti-enumeration guarantee of prelogin (spec 01 ┬з3, spec 05 T11) rests entirely on this secret being unknown: `fakeSalt = HMAC-SHA256(enumSecret, lowercase(email))[0..15]` must be indistinguishable from a real user's random 16-byte salt.
*Failure scenario:* prod deploys without the secret set (easy omission, no error); an attacker computes `HMAC-SHA256(0^32, lowercase(email))[0..15]` locally and calls `POST /auth/prelogin` тАФ responses whose `kdfSalt` equals the computed value are unregistered, all others are real accounts. On a break-glass public window (`prelogin` is only 10/min IP-limited, not locked down), this is internet-facing user enumeration.
*Scope:* enumeration only; no ZK/credential impact. Tailnet-normal, meaningful only during a rare public window against a tiny, already-known email set.
*Remediation:* fail startup (`require`) when `ANDVARI_ENUM_SECRET` is absent/empty/short in production; treat a 0-byte key as fatal, not a default.

**LOW-5 тАФ Non-transactional attachment file deletion diverges from batch rollback (dangling item тЖТ missing blob)**
`server/.../AttachmentStore.kt:127` (batch tx at `Service.kt:273`; rollback at `Db.kt:30-42`; 403 symptom at `App.kt:306`).
A whole push batch runs in one SQLite transaction that rolls back on any throwable. But `applyDelete` тЖТ `deleteForItem` does a transactional `DELETE FROM attachments` followed by a **non-transactional** filesystem delete `ids.forEach { file(it).delete() }`. If a *later* mutation in the same batch throws (`put_without_item` / `unknown_attachment` / `attachment_mismatch` / quota тАФ all client-reachable), the tx rolls back and restores the item and attachment rows, but the blob file is already gone. `GET /attachments/{id}` then 403s on `!blob.isFile`. There is no in-app recovery (`item_versions` archives only the envelope, not attachment ciphertext).
*Failure scenario:* client pushes `[delete X (owns attachment A), put Y referencing not-yet-uploaded B]`; delete-X unlinks A's file, put-Y throws, tx rolls back тЖТ X live again but A's ciphertext permanently gone. If X is edited elsewhere before the retry (edit-beats-delete, `Service.kt:339`), X stays live referencing an irrecoverable A.
*Scope:* integrity/availability of one attachment, not confidentiality; loss only *persists* if the delete ultimately never commits (a narrow race or a persistently-broken client); the symptom is a visible 403, and PBS blob-dir backups give a recovery path within retention.
*Remediation:* collect ids from `deleteForItem` and unlink files in a **post-commit** hook (only after the batch tx commits), or defer all file removal to the orphan GC sweep.

**LOW-6 тАФ Streamed `.part` temp files are not counted against the per-user quota**
`server/.../AttachmentStore.kt:64` (per-attachment cap `:85`; committed-only quota check `:102`; cleanup `:118`).
Upload bodies stream to `{attachmentId}.part` *outside* the DB lock; only the final commit is serialized. Each in-flight upload is bounded only by the ~25 MiB per-attachment cap, and the 1 GiB `userAttachmentsMaxBytes` quota is checked against **committed rows only** тАФ bytes in `.part` files are uncounted. The upload route has no rate limiter and no concurrency cap (`App.kt:285`); Netty has no request-read timeout, so held-open connections keep space occupied.
*Failure scenario:* a household member's compromised/malicious client opens hundreds of concurrent slow uploads (~25 MiB each); several GiB of `.part` files accumulate under `blobDir`, none counted against the 1 GiB cap, filling the filesystem shared with `andvari.db` and stalling SQLite writes for all users until connections drop.
*Scope:* requires an authenticated writer; transient and self-healing; spec 05 R5/T1 already accept insider/availability DoS.
*Remediation:* bound concurrent in-flight uploads per user (a per-user semaphore) and/or count active `.part` sizes toward the quota; add a request-read timeout so slow-loris uploads are reaped.

**LOW-7 тАФ Denied sync-push mutations are never audited (spec 03 ┬з5 violation)**
`server/.../Service.kt:289`.
spec 03 ┬з5 requires a `denied` push result (reader pushing to a shared vault, or no grant) to be *"audited."* In `applyMutation`, when `grantRole` returns `null` or `"reader"`, the code returns `MutationResult(mutationId, "denied")` and falls straight through to write only the mutation-dedup row тАФ it never calls `auditOn`/`audit`. Every *other* security-relevant action (login, login_fail, totp, password_change, invite, disable, revoke, recovery) writes an audit row, so unauthorized write attempts are the only class silently dropped from the trail. The only trace is a transient dedup row that `auditQuery` does not read and that is GC'd within the тЙе24 h/1000-mutation window.
*Failure scenario:* a revoked/reader member (or a holder of a still-valid access token whose grant was revoked) pushes a `put`; the server returns `denied` and leaves no forensic record in the audit table or Loki тАФ exactly the intrusion event the spec promises to log.
*Remediation:* call `auditOn(c, "push_denied", userId, deviceId, ip, meta=vaultId/itemId)` on the denied branch before returning.

**LOW-8 тАФ `PUT /escrow/self` mutates the recovery-critical escrow blob with no audit row**
`server/.../App.kt:333`.
The `/escrow/self` route upserts the user's sealed UVK escrow blob inside a `db.tx` but never audits. Escrow is the sole recovery path for a forgotten master password (spec 04); overwriting it is security-relevant, yet the admin-side `recovery_apply` is audited while the self-upload that establishes/replaces the blob is not. The route's only guard is `fingerprint == config.recoveryFingerprint` (line 332) тАФ but `fingerprint` is client-supplied plaintext not derived from `sealed`, and by ZK design the server cannot validate `sealed`'s contents (only offline `recovery-cli`'s sha256 self-check can). So a garbage `sealed` carrying the publicly-known org fingerprint is accepted.
*Failure scenario:* a holder of a live access token PUTs `/escrow/self` with the org fingerprint and a garbage payload; the stored blob is silently replaced, and a later admin recovery attempt fails the offline sha256 check тАФ the account's forgot-password recovery path is broken, with no audit trail of when/who tampered. (Vault data is not lost тАФ the UVK persists client-side тАФ so this is a narrow recovery-path DoS + forensic gap.)
*Remediation:* audit `escrow_self_upload` inside the same tx (as `register`'s escrow write already is), recording userId/deviceId/fingerprint.

**LOW-9 тАФ WebSocket dirty-bell passes the access token in the URL query string**
`web/src/api/client.ts:259` (server accepts it at `App.kt:407-414`).
`events()` connects to `.../api/v1/events?access=${accessToken}`, embedding the bearer token in the request URL. Browsers can't set headers on a WS handshake, so query-param auth is a common necessary pattern and it does **not** widen the trust surface over TLS (no ZK impact). The concrete concern is log hygiene: query strings are far likelier than an `Authorization` header to be persisted verbatim тАФ and Cloudflare's HTTP request logs capture URI+query while redacting `Authorization`.
*Verification note:* the server itself does *not* log it (no `CallLogging`; `io.ktor` stays at INFO), so exposure is narrowly scoped to the external CF edge on the public break-glass path. Access TTL is 3600 s and spec 03 ┬з8 forbids refresh on the public origin, so a replayed token yields ciphertext + metadata (not plaintext) for тЙд1 h.
*Failure scenario:* break-glass login over the tunnel; the edge access log captures the full WS URL with the live token; anyone who can later read those logs replays it until expiry.
*Remediation:* switch WS auth to a short-lived single-use ticket minted over the authenticated REST channel and passed in the query (so the URL-borne secret is one-time and seconds-lived), or use the `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` subprotocol header to carry the token.

### INFO (8)

**INFO-1 тАФ Account email written into audit `meta` and shipped to Loki** тАФ `server/.../Service.kt:101` (also `AdminService.kt:40`; emitted at `AuditLog.kt:24`; routed by `logback.xml:8-12` тЖТ stdout тЖТ journald тЖТ Alloy тЖТ Loki per spec 03 ┬з7). `register`/`invite_create` place the raw email into `meta`. Not a ZK break (email is an explicit ┬з5 `users` field) and within the letter of ┬з5's audit-row constraint, but `userId` already identifies the actor, so the email adds no audit value while copying household members' addresses into the central monitoring store (VM 112 тАФ a different trust boundary and retention than CT 122). *Remediation:* drop `email` from these two `meta` payloads; log `userId` only.

**INFO-2 тАФ Web escrow fingerprint is entirely server-sourced, confirmed by a bare checkbox** тАФ `web/src/ui/Welcome.tsx:255` (pubkey from server `:270-276`, fingerprint from server policy `:186/:202`; unused `shortFingerprint()` exists at `web/src/crypto/escrow.ts:61-63`). The core check proves only that the server's pubkey matches the server's own fingerprint; spec 04 ┬з2 point 3 requires *"checkbox + short-form entry,"* but the UI implements only the checkbox, and there is no build-time pinned fingerprint for the web client. *Why only INFO:* the sole adversary who can exploit this тАФ a malicious server serving a substituted recovery pubkey тАФ already dominates the web client by serving hostile JS that captures the master password directly (spec 05 T6/R2, an accepted gap); native apps are the designated trust anchor. *Remediation:* add the mandated short-form entry step in the web enrollment flow (the helper already exists); prefer keeping high-value escrow confirmation on native clients.

**INFO-3 тАФ prelogin returns real per-user `kdfParams` for known accounts but default params for unknown** тАФ `server/.../Service.kt:51` (vs `:53`). The fake salt masks account existence, but the `kdfParams` field does not: the moment any account's params diverge from the compile-time default (a future KDF upgrade per spec 01 ┬з7, or an operator policy bump тАФ line 53 uses hardcoded `ClientPolicy()` rather than the stored policy), a non-default response definitively marks a real account. Currently **inert** (P0 kept every account on the default), so this is a latent oracle, not a live one. *Remediation:* return the *current default policy* params for both known and unknown emails at the prelogin step (params still ride inside `wrappedUvk` for the authenticated client), or otherwise make the params response uniform.

**INFO-4 тАФ Idempotency re-upload check is a cross-tenant attachment-existence oracle** тАФ `server/.../AttachmentStore.kt:99` (`rowById` is globally scoped, `:34`; route checks caller-supplied vaultId, `App.kt:291`). A re-upload of a *taken* `attachmentId` with differing `(sha256,itemId,vaultId)` returns `400 attachment_id_taken`, distinguishing "this exact id exists somewhere" from "it's free." Negligible: ids are client-generated 122-bit random UUIDs (spec 02 ┬з1) an attacker cannot guess, and the error is uniform across which field differs, so no binding/content leaks. No fix required; recorded per the audit's explicit request to assess the oracle.

**INFO-5 тАФ `policy_update` audit is written in a separate transaction from the policy change** тАФ `server/.../App.kt:402` (`setPolicyJson` commits its own tx at `Repo.kt:267-269`; audit opens a second tx at `Repo.kt:246-248`). Unlike every in-tx admin action (invite/disable/revoke/recovery thread `auditOn` through the mutating tx), the policy write and its audit are non-atomic; a crash in the microsecond window between the two commits leaves the org policy changed with no audit row (spec 03 ┬з7 "every call audited"). Not adversary-controllable and not a suppression primitive. *Remediation:* thread `auditOn` through `setPolicy`'s transaction.

**INFO-6 тАФ Admin `disable`/`revoke`/`escrow` path ids are not UUID-validated** тАФ `server/.../App.kt:361` (`:366`, `:371`; contrast `:389` and attachment routes, which use `requireUuid`). Ids flow only into parameterized `WHERE userId=?/deviceId=?` statements тАФ **not SQL-injectable, not traversal** тАФ so this is purely a consistency/defense-in-depth nit; a malformed id yields a silent 200 no-op `UPDATE` on `disable`/`revoke` (the escrow route already 400s). *Remediation:* apply `requireUuid` to these three ids for a clean 400 and consistency.

**INFO-7 тАФ Session access + refresh tokens persisted in `localStorage`** тАФ `web/src/ui/session.ts:31` (acknowledged as an MVP choice in the file). Standard bearer-token web tradeoff; **does not break ZK** тАФ the UVK/keys are never persisted (`web/src/vault/account.ts`), so a `localStorage` leak yields ciphertext-sync/metadata only. Verified there is **no in-app XSS sink** (no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`/`innerHTML`/`eval`/injectable `href`/`src` in `web/src`); the only readers are malware (T5) or a hostile origin (T6) тАФ both already in spec 05's accepted set. Recorded for exactness: tokens yes, keys no.

**INFO-8 тАФ CSP allows `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` and omits `object-src`/`form-action`** тАФ `server/.../App.kt:436`. The CSP is correctly strict where it matters (`script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'`, `connect-src 'self'`, `img-src 'self' data:`, `base-uri 'none'`, `frame-ancestors 'none'`). Gaps: `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` (React inline `style={{}}` + a `--p` custom prop), `object-src` unset (falls back to `default-src 'self'`, not `'none'`), `form-action` unset (does not inherit `default-src`). **Non-exploitable today** тАФ there is no HTML-injection sink and `script-src` blocks inline JS. *Remediation:* add `object-src 'none'`, `form-action 'none'`, and (if feasible) replace inline styles to drop `'unsafe-inline'` тАФ pure defense-in-depth against a future sink.

---

## 4. Accepted risks (spec 05) that surfaced

Several findings are the visible edge of risks the owner has already signed off at the hardening gate. Naming them explicitly so they are not re-litigated:

- **R2 тАФ Web client page-load trust (T6):** the backstop under INFO-2 (server-sourced escrow fingerprint), INFO-7 (tokens in `localStorage`), and INFO-8 (CSP tightening). A hostile web origin is accepted as game-over; native apps are the trust anchor and high-value operations (the escrow genesis ceremony) never run in the web client. These findings are hardening *within* an accepted gap, not new exposure.
- **R4 тАФ Server metadata visibility (A6):** the residual behind INFO-3 (prelogin params oracle), INFO-4 (attachment-existence oracle), and the enumeration surface of LOW-4/LOW-3. Who-syncs-when, item/attachment counts and sizes, and existence of unguessable ids are visible to the server by nature and accepted.
- **R5 тАФ Single server, availability via offline-first + PBS/shadow-2122, not HA:** the context for the attachment integrity/availability findings LOW-5 and LOW-6 (blob loss and temp-file DoS are bounded by offline caches and PBS recovery, not prevented by redundancy).
- **R1 тАФ JVM/JS cannot guarantee secret zeroization;** **R3 тАФ the escrow key holder can decrypt every account** (deliberate тАФ it *is* the recovery feature, spec 04); **R6 тАФ TOTP seeds co-located with passwords.** These did not produce findings but remain the standing accepted posture and were confirmed unchanged by the code.

Note that LOW-1 (KDF floor) sits *against* an accepted risk rather than within one: spec 05 T8 explicitly says "policy enforces minimum strength at enrollment." The code does not, so this is a genuine deviation from the accepted-risk's own stated mitigation тАФ it should be fixed, not accepted.

---

## 5. Hardening-gate status

| Gate item | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Self-audit** | тЬЕ **Cleared by this report** | Server + `:core`/web crypto + auth/session + attachments + admin + web client covered; findings adversarially verified. |
| **Threat-model conformance** | ЁЯЯб **Conditional pass** | The ZK contract (T1/T7) holds in code; no critical/high/medium survived. **One blocker:** LOW-1 violates the spec 05 T8 mitigation ("policy enforces minimum strength at enrollment"). Mark conformant only after the KDF floor is enforced. |
| **PBS restore drill** | тЫФ **Not cleared** | Operational; out of scope for a code audit. Must be exercised on CT 122's DB + `blobDir` before real secrets. LOW-5 makes the blob-dir restore path load-bearing. |
| **Escrow recovery drill** | тЫФ **Not cleared тАФ hard blocker** | The offline escrow **genesis ceremony** (keygen тЖТ sheet ├Ч2 + USB тЖТ pin pubkey тЖТ canary make/verify from the printed sheet) is still outstanding; enrollment returns **503 until it is done**. Run the canary end-to-end + a full drill-account recovery per spec 04 ┬з4 before the first real account. |
| **Min-version pin exercised** | тЫФ **Not cleared** | The `426 upgrade_required` path (spec 03 ┬з1) exists in code but must be exercised end-to-end (bump `minVersion`, confirm all three clients block writes and show the upgrade path) before relying on it as a kill-switch. |
| **30-day soak** | тЫФ **Not started** | Time-based; begins after the code fixes land and the drills pass. |

**What this audit clears:** the self-audit gate item, and a strong evidentiary basis that the zero-knowledge invariant and the T1/T7/T8 confidentiality guarantees hold in the implementation.

**What still blocks real-secret migration:**
1. **Fix-first (before first enrollment):** LOW-1 (KDF floor тАФ also unblocks the threat-model-conformance gate), LOW-2 (one-line refresh-token guard), LOW-4 (enum-secret startup guard), LOW-5 (post-commit attachment unlink). LOW-7/LOW-8 (audit the `denied` and `escrow/self` paths) are small and worth landing in the same pass.
2. **Operational gates the code cannot clear:** the escrow genesis + recovery drill (hard blocker тАФ enrollment is 503 until the ceremony runs), the PBS restore drill, the min-version-pin exercise, and the 30-day soak.

No critical, high, or medium code defect stands between andvari and the real-secrets migration тАФ the remaining work is a short list of low/info hardening fixes plus the operational drills.

---

## 6. Remediation status тАФ 2026-07-06 (same day)

The audit's **fix-first list** (server-side hardening to land before real-secret migration) was applied and verified the same day (`scripts/verify.sh` green: server + core + web):

| Finding | Fix | Where |
|---|---|---|
| **LOW-1** KDF floor not enforced | Server now rejects enrollment/re-key params below a config floor (`400 kdf_too_weak`); production floor 64 MiB / ops 3 via `fromEnv`, off (0/0) under the test config so fast-param tests are unaffected. | `Config.kt`, `Service.register`/`changePassword` |
| **LOW-2** refresh-token TOCTOU | Consume is now a guarded CAS (`UPDATE тАж WHERE refreshConsumedAt IS NULL`) with an affected-row check тЖТ a concurrent second use hits the reuse branch (device revoked), mirroring the TOTP guarded-step. | `Service.refresh` |
| **LOW-4** enum secret silent zero-default | `Config.fromEnv` now **fails startup** if `ANDVARI_ENUM_SECRET` is unset/blank/<32 bytes (fail-closed). | `Config.kt`, `ops/andvari.env.example` |
| **LOW-5** non-transactional attachment unlink | Blob files are unlinked **only after the batch tx commits**; a rolled-back batch no longer orphans a live item's ciphertext. | `Service.push`/`applyDelete`, `AttachmentStore.deleteRowsForItem` |
| **LOW-7** denied push not audited | `push_denied` audit row now written in-tx on the denied branch. | `Service.applyMutation` |
| **LOW-8** escrow self-upload not audited | `escrow_self_upload` audit row now written in the escrow tx. | `App.kt` `/escrow/self` |

**Threat-model conformance** (┬з5 gate) is now unblocked: the spec 05 T8 mitigation ("policy enforces minimum strength at enrollment") is implemented. The **operational** gate items (escrow genesis + recovery drill, PBS restore drill, min-version-pin exercise, 30-day soak) remain owner/ops work and still gate real-secret migration.

### 6b. Remaining LOW/INFO тАФ done 2026-07-06 (second hardening pass, deployed CT 122)

The defense-in-depth / hygiene items are now implemented, tested, and live (an 11-agent code-review pass verified the batch and caught two accounting bugs, folded in):

| Finding | Fix | Where |
|---|---|---|
| **LOW-3** proxy client-IP | `clientIp()` trusts `CF-Connecting-IP` / rightmost `X-Forwarded-For` **only when the direct peer is loopback** (shared `peerIsLoopback()` with the `/metrics` gate); IP-literal + non-wildcard guard; `ANDVARI_TRUSTED_IP_HEADERS` env-configurable. | `Auth.kt`, `App.kt`, `Config.kt` |
| **LOW-6** in-flight upload quota | Per-user concurrency semaphore + streamed `.part` bytes counted toward the user quota mid-stream (idempotent re-uploads exempt; bytes handed off before commit); Netty `requestReadTimeoutSeconds` (default **off** until idle-WS survival is verified live). | `AttachmentStore.kt`, `Config.kt`, `Main.kt` |
| **LOW-9** WS token-in-URL | Single-use ~30 s ticket minted over authenticated REST (`POST /events/ticket`); raw `?access=` token removed; Bearer-header path kept. тЙд30 s revoked-ticket window recorded in spec 03 ┬з6. | `WsTicketStore.kt`, `App.kt`, web `client.ts` |
| **INFO-1** email in audit meta | Meta carries the invite token-hash prefix, never the email (createтЖФredeem still correlate). | `Service.kt`, `AdminService.kt` |
| **INFO-2** web escrow short-form entry | Enroll requires typing the fingerprint short form from the printed sheet before it is revealed; spec 04 ┬з2(3) updated to match (entry-first, not display-first). | web `Welcome.tsx`, `escrow.ts` |
| **INFO-3** prelogin params | Unknown emails get the current org-default params, computed at uniform cost across both branches (no timing oracle); residual per-user divergence recorded in spec 05 R4. | `Service.kt` |
| **INFO-5** policy audit atomicity | `policy_update` audited inside the policy write tx. | `Service.kt`, `Repo.kt`, `App.kt` |
| **INFO-6** admin id validation | `requireUuid` on disable / revoke / escrow-GET ids. | `App.kt` |
| **INFO-8** CSP | `object-src 'none'` + `form-action 'none'` added; `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` documented-and-kept. | `App.kt` |

**Owner smoke follow-up (non-blocking):** confirm which forwarded header `tailscale serve` sets (a tailnet login should show a `100.x` address in the audit `ip`); if the request-read timeout is later enabled, confirm an idle web WebSocket survives past it (ping keepalive).
